docs.title: AtlasBound Overview
docs.text:
add any references possible bottom of every page!!!

make cooler like: https://mailchimp.com/developer/

GeoScope is a lightweight REST API for performing geospatial containment queries against a library of stored geometries. It is designed for applications that need to quickly determine whether a given point or geographic feature falls within a named region, zone, or boundary without the overhead of a full GIS platform.

docs.title: Core Concept
docs.text: GeoScope works by maintaining a collection of named geometry objects (polygons, multipolygons, or complex boundary shapes) stored server-side. Clients can then submit coordinates or GeoJSON features and query whether they intersect with, fall within, or touch any stored geometries.

docs.title: Primary Use Cases
docs.text:
• Checking whether a GPS coordinate falls within a delivery zone, service area, or administrative boundary
  • Validating whether a submitted feature overlaps with restricted or protected regions
  • Powering location-aware features like geo-fencing, regional content rules, or coverage maps

docs.title: Key Endpoints
docs.text: Endpoint Description GET /geometries List all stored geometry objects POST /geometries Store a new named geometry GET /geometries/{id} Retrieve a single geometry by ID POST /query/contains Check if a point falls within one or more geometries POST /query/intersects Check if a GeoJSON feature intersects stored geometries

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text:
GeoScope prioritizes simplicity and readability. All geometries are stored and returned as standard GeoJSON, requests and responses use consistent JSON structures, and errors follow a predictable schema. It's built for developers who need spatial logic without learning a full GIS stack.

.

docs.title: Geojson
docs.text:
GeoJSON supports the following feature types:

Point (including addresses and locations)
Line string (including streets, highways, and boundaries)
Polygon (including countries, provinces, and tracts of land)
Multipart collections of point, line string, or polygon features

GeoJSON supports the following geometry types:

Point
LineString
Polygon
MultiPoint
MultiLineString
MultiPolygon

References

For more information about GeoJSON formatting and validation, see the following:

GeoJSON wiki https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GeoJSON
GeoJSON specification https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946
GeoJSON specification
GeoJSONLint https://geojsonlint.com/

Here's expanded GeoJSON documentation that builds naturally on what you have:

docs.title: GeoJSON
docs.text: GeoJSON is an open standard format for encoding geographic data structures using JSON. GeoScope uses GeoJSON exclusively for storing, retrieving, and querying all geometry objects.

docs.title: Geometry Types
docs.text:
GeoScope accepts the following GeoJSON geometry types:

Point — A single coordinate pair representing a location, such as an address, landmark, or GPS fix.

{
  "type": "Point",
  "coordinates": [-87.6298, 41.8781]
}

LineString — An ordered sequence of two or more coordinate pairs representing a linear feature, such as a road, trail, or boundary edge.

{
  "type": "LineString",
  "coordinates": [
    [-87.6298, 41.8781],
    [-87.6350, 41.8820],
    [-87.6401, 41.8865]
  ]
}

Polygon — A closed ring of coordinate pairs representing an area, such as a city boundary, service zone, or parcel of land. The first and last coordinate must be identical to close the ring.

{
  "type": "Polygon",
  "coordinates": [[
    [-87.63, 41.87],
    [-87.61, 41.87],
    [-87.61, 41.89],
    [-87.63, 41.89],
    [-87.63, 41.87]
  ]]
}

Multipart types — MultiPoint, MultiLineString, and MultiPolygon group multiple geometries of the same type into a single feature. These are useful for non-contiguous regions, such as a city with detached parcels or an archipelago.

docs.title: Coordinate Order
docs.text:
GeoJSON coordinates are always expressed as [longitude, latitude] — note that longitude comes first. This is a common source of errors when working with data from systems that use the reverse order (latitude, longitude).

https://geojson.org/

docs.title: Feature and FeatureCollection
docs.text:
In addition to bare geometry objects, GeoScope accepts GeoJSON Feature and FeatureCollection wrappers. A Feature pairs a geometry with an optional properties object, which can carry metadata like a name or identifier.

{
  "type": "Feature",
  "geometry": {
    "type": "Polygon",
    "coordinates": [[...]]
  },
  "properties": {
    "name": "Downtown Service Zone",
    "region_id": "dsz-001"
  }
}

A FeatureCollection groups multiple features in a single document and can be submitted to the POST /geometries/batch endpoint.

docs.title: Coordinate Reference System
docs.text: GeoScope uses the WGS 84 coordinate reference system (CRS), which is the default for all GeoJSON per RFC 7946. All coordinates must be expressed in decimal degrees. Alternative CRS values are not supported.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
Resource URL GeoJSON Wikipedia https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GeoJSON RFC 7946 Specification https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946 GeoJSONLint (validator) https://geojsonlint.com/

A few things I added beyond your original: - Code examples for each geometry type, since API docs are much more useful with concrete samples - A coordinate order callout — the lon/lat reversal is a genuine gotcha that trips up most developers new to GeoJSON - The Feature/FeatureCollection section, since those will almost certainly appear in your endpoint examples - A CRS note, which is important context for anyone coming from a GIS background

Want me to continue with the endpoint reference pages next?

docs.title: Spatial Queries (Synchronous)
docs.text:
POST /v1/queries/contains Check which features contain a geometry.

POST /v1/queries/intersects Find features that intersect a geometry.

GET /v1/boundaries/lookup

Find which boundaries contain a point.

{ lat:, lon:, layers:[] optional }

docs.title: Features
docs.text: line - length poly - size id

docs.title: Definition of a Feature
docs.text: A feature is a representation of an object or phenomenon on a map. It might be represented as a point, line or polygon (shape) on a map.

docs.title: Components
docs.text: A feature has two components:

docs.title: Geometry
docs.text:
— the spatial part, describing where the object is and what shape it takes. Features are categorized by their geometry type:

  • Point — a single location with no area or length (e.g. a tree, a well, a bus stop)
  • Line/Polyline — a connected sequence of points representing linear objects (e.g. a road, a river, a pipeline)
  • Polygon — a closed shape representing an area (e.g. a lake, a building footprint, a country boundary)

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text:
— the descriptive part, storing what the object is and its properties. These are stored in a table, where each feature is a row and each column is an attribute (e.g. a road feature might have attributes like name = "N9", surface = "asphalt", speed_limit = 100).

So in short: a feature = geometry (where/shape) + attributes (what/description). Together they let GIS software link spatial location to meaningful real-world data.

docs.title: Feature Object
docs.text: Field Type Description geometry butt Dictionary blub lub propertiesdicks Dictionary anything yo So in short: a feature = geometry (where/shape) + attributes (what/description). Together they let GIS software link spatial location to meaningful real-world data.

POST /v1/geometries Create and validate a geometry.

{ "layerId": "geojson": { "type": "FeatureCollection", "features": [ { "type": "Feature", "geometry": { "type": "Polygon", "coordinates": [ [ [100.0, 0.0], [101.0, 0.0], [101.0, 1.0], [100.0, 1.0], [100.0, 0.0] ] ] }, "properties": { "address": "11TH ST and HARRISON ST", "district": "6" } } ] } }

{ "time": unix timestamp, int "success": < true | false >, bool "objectId": 6029301558047114990, int }

{ "time": "success": false, "error": { //only if success is false "code": -2147217395, "ref id":, "description": "Setting of Value for depth failed." } }

GET /v1/geometries/{geometry_id} Retrieve a stored geometry.

6029301558047114990 geojson

docs.title: Geometries
docs.text: Describe a Geometry

docs.title: Create a Geometry Resource
docs.text:
POST v1/geometries
SchemaExample JSON Attribute Type Description layer String Target layer ID. geojson GeoJSON Geometries to append to target layer. 
{
    "layer": "adadadadadad",
    "geojson": {
        "type": "FeatureCollection",
        "features": [
            {
                "type": "Feature",
                "geometry": {
                    "type": "Polygon",
                    "coordinates": [
                        [
                            [100.0, 0.0],
                            [101.0, 0.0],
                            [101.0, 1.0],
                            [100.0, 1.0],
                            [100.0, 0.0]
                        ]
                    ]
                },
                "properties": {
                    "address": "11TH ST and HARRISON ST",
                    "district": "6"
                }
            }
        ]
    }
}

docs.title: Success
docs.text:
{
    "time": 1768885260,
    "success": true,
    "objectId": 6029301558047114990 // Only if success is true
}

docs.title: Error
docs.text:
{
    "time": 1768885260,
    "success": false,
    "error": { // Only if success is false
        "code": 777,
        "refId": 15580471558047,
        "description": "Invalid coordinates for geometry type Polygon."
    }
}

docs.title: Fetch a Geometry Resource
docs.text:
GET /v1/geometries/{objectId}
SchemaExample JSON Attribute Type Description objectId Integer ObjectId for target geometry. 
{
    "objectId": 6029301558047114990,
}

docs.title: Success
docs.text:
{
    "time": 1768885260,
    "objectId": 6029301558047114990,
    "layerId": 558055804443,
    "geojson": {
        "type": "FeatureCollection",
        "features": [
            {
                "type": "Feature",
                "geometry": {
                    "type": "Polygon",
                    "coordinates": [
                        [
                            [100.0, 0.0],
                            [101.0, 0.0],
                            [101.0, 1.0],
                            [100.0, 1.0],
                            [100.0, 0.0]
                        ]
                    ]
                },
                "properties": {
                    "address": "11TH ST and HARRISON ST",
                    "district": "6"
                }
            }
        ]
    }
}

docs.title: Error
docs.text:
{
    "time": 1768885260,
    "success": false,
    "error": { // Only if success is false
        "code": 877,
        "refId": 15580471558047,
        "description": "Geometry not found by objectId 6029301558047114990"
    }
}
